title: Oh, Tell Me, Ye Breezes
content:
آ آ آ آ Oh, tell me, ye breezes that spring from the west,
آ آ آ آ Oh, tell me, ere passing away,
آ آ آ آ If Leichhardtï؟½s bold spirit has fled to its rest?
آ آ آ آ Where moulders the travellerï؟½s clay?

آ آ آ آ Perchance as ye flitted on heedlessly by
آ آ آ آ The long lost was yielding his breath;
آ آ آ آ Perchance ye have borne on your wings the last sigh
آ آ آ آ That ï؟½scapï؟½d from the lone one in death.

آ آ آ آ Tell me, ye breezes, yeï؟½ve traversed the wild,
آ آ آ آ And passed oï؟½er the desolate spot,
آ آ آ آ Where reposeth in silence sweet Natureï؟½s own child,
آ آ آ آ Where slumbers one nearly forgot?

آ آ آ آ Ye answer me not but are passing away
آ آ آ آ Ye breezes that spring from the west,
آ آ آ آ Unhallowï؟½d still moulders the travellerï؟½s clay,
آ آ آ آ For unknown is the place of his rest.
meaning: About: Henry Clarence Kendall was an important nineteenth century Australian poet.
